ucs.title: [LLM] Velvet Ant PlugX DLL search-order hijack: signed loader + sideloaded DLL + encrypted .DAT triplet on legacy servers
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical Velvet Ant (G1047) persistence pattern documented by Sygnia: a legitimate signed vendor/Microsoft EXE (e.g. a McAfee, Symantec, or VMware binary) is dropped into a non-system directory together with a small unsigned DLL and an encrypted shellcode payload (typically *.dat / *.bin). The signed loader is launched from disk, side-loads the malicious DLL via search order, which then decrypts the payload — the same sideloading triplet Sygnia attributes to Velvet Ant's long-running PlugX deployments on legacy Windows Server 2003/2008 footholds.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name!="*\\Program Files*" Processes.process_name!="*\\Windows\\*" (Processes.process_signature="Microsoft*" OR Processes.process_signature="McAfee*" OR Processes.process_signature="Symantec*" OR Processes.process_signature="VMware*" OR Processes.process_signature="Hewlett-Packard*") Processes.parent_process_path IN ("*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*","*\\Intel\\*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_path Processes.process_name Processes.process_id
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| join type=inner host process_id [
    | tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_path) as loaded_dll values(Filesystem.file_name) as files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dat" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.bin" by host Filesystem.process_id Filesystem.file_path
    | rename Filesystem.process_id as process_id Filesystem.file_path as loaded_path]
| where like(loaded_dll,"%\\ProgramData\\%") OR like(loaded_dll,"%\\PerfLogs\\%") OR like(loaded_dll,"%\\Users\\Public\\%") OR like(loaded_dll,"%\\Intel\\%")
| where mvcount(files)>=2
| table firstSeen lastSeen host user process_path process_name parent loaded_dll files cmdline
ucs.kql:
// Velvet Ant PlugX sideloading triplet: signed loader in a non-system path loading an unsigned DLL co-located with an encrypted payload
let _suspect_dirs = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\Intel\", @"\RECYCLER\"]);
let _legit_publishers = dynamic(["Microsoft Corporation","McAfee, LLC","McAfee, Inc.","Symantec Corporation","VMware, Inc.","Hewlett-Packard","Hewlett Packard Enterprise","Kaspersky Lab","Trend Micro Inc."]);
let SignedLoader =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | where ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName in (_legit_publishers)
    | where FolderPath has_any (_suspect_dirs)              // signed binary launched from non-system dir
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              LoaderPath = FolderPath, LoaderFile = FileName,
              LoaderSHA256 = SHA256, LoaderPid = ProcessId, LoaderCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
let SideloadedDll =
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName endswith ".dll"
    | where FolderPath has_any (_suspect_dirs)
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
    | project DllTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, LoaderPid = InitiatingProcessId,
              DllPath = FolderPath, DllName = FileName, DllSHA256 = SHA256;
let EncryptedPayload =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
    | where FileName matches regex @"(?i)\.(dat|bin|cfg|wll|ini|tmp)$"
    | where FolderPath has_any (_suspect_dirs)
    | project PayloadTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, PayloadFile = FileName,
              PayloadPath = FolderPath, PayloadSHA256 = SHA256,
              DropperImage = InitiatingProcessFileName;
SignedLoader
| join kind=inner SideloadedDll on DeviceId, LoaderPid
| join kind=inner EncryptedPayload on DeviceId
| where PayloadPath =~ LoaderPath                          // triplet co-located in same dir
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            Payloads = make_set(PayloadFile, 25),
            DllSet = make_set(DllName, 10),
            CmdLines = make_set(LoaderCmd, 5)
            by DeviceName, AccountName, LoaderFile, LoaderPath, LoaderSHA256, DllName, DllSHA256
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Velvet Ant PlugX sideloading triplet — Sentinel via Sysmon (EID 1 + EID 7) and ASIM file events
let _suspect_dirs = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\Intel\", @"\RECYCLER\"]);
let _legit_publishers = dynamic(["Microsoft Corporation","McAfee, LLC","McAfee, Inc.","Symantec Corporation","VMware, Inc.","Hewlett-Packard","Hewlett Packard Enterprise","Kaspersky Lab","Trend Micro Inc."]);
let SignedLoader =
    WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 1
    | extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
             Company = tostring(EventData.Company),
             ProcGuid = tostring(EventData.ProcessGuid),
             User = tostring(EventData.User),
             CmdLine = tostring(EventData.CommandLine),
             Hashes = tostring(EventData.Hashes)
    | where Company in (_legit_publishers)
    | where Image has_any (_suspect_dirs)
    | project LoaderTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, User, Image, CmdLine, ProcGuid, Hashes;
let SideloadedDll =
    WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
    | extend ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
             ProcGuid = tostring(EventData.ProcessGuid),
             Signed = tostring(EventData.Signed),
             Signature = tostring(EventData.Signature)
    | where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
    | where ImageLoaded has_any (_suspect_dirs)
    | where ImageLoaded !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
    | where Signed =~ "false" or Signature == ""
    | project DllTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, ProcGuid, ImageLoaded;
let EncryptedPayload =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
    | where TargetFileName matches regex @"(?i)\.(dat|bin|cfg|wll|ini|tmp)$"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (_suspect_dirs)
    | project PayloadTime = TimeGenerated, Computer = DvcHostname,
              TargetFilePath, TargetFileName;
SignedLoader
| join kind=inner SideloadedDll on Computer, ProcGuid
| join kind=inner EncryptedPayload on Computer
| extend LoaderDir = tostring(split(Image, "\\")[-2])
| where TargetFilePath has LoaderDir
| summarize FirstSeen = min(LoaderTime), LastSeen = max(LoaderTime),
            Payloads = make_set(TargetFileName, 25),
            Dlls = make_set(ImageLoaded, 10)
            by Computer, User, Image, CmdLine
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Velvet Ant PlugX Signed-Loader Sideloading Triplet
id: 7d1f3b6a-2c0e-4ab4-9b1f-velvetant001
status: experimental
description: Detects Velvet Ant (G1047) PlugX execution chain — a legitimately-signed vendor binary executed from a non-system directory adjacent to an unsigned DLL and an encrypted payload file (*.dat/*.bin/*.cfg).
references:
  - https://www.sygnia.co/blog/china-nexus-threat-group-velvet-ant
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1574.001
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g1047
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_signed:
    Company:
      - 'Microsoft Corporation'
      - 'McAfee, LLC'
      - 'McAfee, Inc.'
      - 'Symantec Corporation'
      - 'VMware, Inc.'
      - 'Hewlett-Packard'
      - 'Kaspersky Lab'
      - 'Trend Micro Inc.'
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
      - '\Intel\'
      - '\RECYCLER\'
  filter_legit:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\'
      - 'C:\Windows\SysWOW64\'
  condition: selection_signed and selection_path and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
  - Vendor installers staging signed binaries to %ProgramData% during update windows
  - Portable admin tooling intentionally executed from %Public%
level: high
ucs.rationale: Velvet Ant's tooling has been documented by Sygnia (2024 reports) running PlugX on long-forgotten legacy Windows servers via the classic three-file sideloading triplet (signed loader EXE + unsigned DLL + encrypted payload). Most APTs do sideloading occasionally; Velvet Ant does it specifically against legacy host beachheads with co-located triplets, which is what the join across DeviceProcessEvent
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Velvet Ant internal C2 pivot from compromised F5 BIG-IP / network appliance to Windows estate (T1090.001 + T1133)
ucs.description: Velvet Ant (G1047) is uniquely characterized by Sygnia for living on F5 BIG-IP and other network appliances for years, then using the appliance as an internal proxy hop back into the Windows estate over non-standard ports — defeating perimeter EDR. This UC hunts inbound TCP from the management subnets that host F5/Cisco/Juniper appliances toward Windows servers on non-standard high ports, paired with subsequent SMB lateral-tool-transfer or WMI/service-create execution from the same source IP within a short window — the exact appliance→server pivot Sygnia attributes to this group.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as ports values(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as bytes_in values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.src_ip IN ("10.10.250.0/24","10.10.251.0/24","192.168.250.0/24") (All_Traffic.dest_port>1024 AND All_Traffic.dest_port!=3389 AND All_Traffic.dest_port!=5985 AND All_Traffic.dest_port!=5986 AND All_Traffic.dest_port!=445) by All_Traffic.src_ip All_Traffic.dest_ip All_Traffic.dest_port
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`
| where bytes_in>1024 AND bytes_out>1024
| join type=inner dest_ip [
    | tstats summariesonly=true values(Processes.process) as wmi_cmd values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="wmiprvse.exe" OR Processes.process_name="services.exe" OR Processes.process_name="sc.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="wmiprvse.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="services.exe") earliest=-30m latest=now by host Processes.process_name
    | rename host as dest_ip]
| join type=inner dest_ip [
    | tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_path) as dropped_files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="*\\ADMIN$*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\C$\\*" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dll") earliest=-30m latest=now by host
    | rename host as dest_ip]
| table firstSeen lastSeen src_ip dest_ip dest_port bytes_in bytes_out wmi_cmd dropped_files user
ucs.kql:
// Velvet Ant pivot: appliance management subnets → Windows estate on non-standard port + SMB drop + WMI/service execution within 30m
let _appliance_mgmt_subnets = dynamic(["10.10.250.0/24","10.10.251.0/24","192.168.250.0/24"]); // F5/Cisco/Juniper mgmt VLANs — tune per env
let _expected_ports = dynamic([445, 3389, 5985, 5986, 22, 161, 514]);
let PivotConn =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("InboundConnectionAccepted","ConnectionSuccess")
    | where ipv4_is_in_any_range(RemoteIP, _appliance_mgmt_subnets)
    | where RemotePort !in (_expected_ports) and LocalPort !in (_expected_ports)
    | where LocalPort > 1024
    | project PivotTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              ApplianceIP = RemoteIP, AppliancePort = RemotePort,
              VictimIP = LocalIP, VictimPort = LocalPort,
              PivotProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName;
let SmbDrop =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".bat" or FileName endswith ".ps1"
    | where RequestProtocol == "SMB2" or FolderPath has_any (@"\ADMIN$", @"\C$\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\")
    | where isnotempty(RequestSourceIP)
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId,
              DroppedFile = FileName, DropPath = FolderPath,
              DropSrcIP = RequestSourceIP, DropSHA256 = SHA256;
let RemoteExec =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("wmiprvse.exe","services.exe","svchost.exe")
          or FileName in~ ("sc.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","reg.exe","schtasks.exe")
          or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe") and InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "wmiprvse.exe")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project ExecTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ExecProcess = FileName, ExecCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ExecParent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
PivotConn
| join kind=inner SmbDrop on DeviceId
| where DropTime between (PivotTime .. PivotTime + 30m)
| where DropSrcIP == ApplianceIP
| join kind=inner RemoteExec on DeviceId
| where ExecTime between (PivotTime .. PivotTime + 30m)
| project PivotTime, DropTime, ExecTime,
          ApplianceIP, AppliancePort, VictimIP = DeviceName,
          AccountName, DroppedFile, DropPath, DropSHA256,
          ExecProcess, ExecParent, ExecCmd
| order by PivotTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Velvet Ant appliance pivot — Sentinel ASIM network + SecurityEvent process-create
let _appliance_mgmt_subnets = dynamic(["10.10.250.0/24","10.10.251.0/24","192.168.250.0/24"]);
let _expected_ports = dynamic([445, 3389, 5985, 5986, 22, 161, 514]);
let PivotConn =
    ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventResult == "Success"
    | where ipv4_is_in_any_range(SrcIpAddr, _appliance_mgmt_subnets)
    | where DstPortNumber !in (_expected_ports)
    | where DstPortNumber > 1024
    | project PivotTime = TimeGenerated, Computer = DstHostname,
              ApplianceIP = SrcIpAddr, AppliancePort = DstPortNumber,
              VictimIP = DstIpAddr;
let RemoteExec =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where ParentProcessName has_any ("wmiprvse.exe","services.exe","svchost.exe")
         or NewProcessName has_any ("\\sc.exe","\\net.exe","\\net1.exe","\\schtasks.exe")
         or (NewProcessName has_any ("\\powershell.exe","\\cmd.exe") and ParentProcessName has "wmiprvse.exe")
    | project ExecTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
let RemoteFileWrite =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 5145    // detailed file share access
    | where ObjectName has_any ("\\ADMIN$","\\C$")
    | where AccessMask has_any ("0x2","0x4","0x100")
    | project ShareTime = TimeGenerated, Computer,
              ShareName = ObjectName, ShareSrcIp = IpAddress,
              ShareUser = Account;
PivotConn
| join kind=inner RemoteFileWrite on Computer
| where ShareTime between (PivotTime .. PivotTime + 30m)
| where ShareSrcIp == ApplianceIP
| join kind=inner RemoteExec on Computer
| where ExecTime between (PivotTime .. PivotTime + 30m)
| project PivotTime, ShareTime, ExecTime,
          ApplianceIP, AppliancePort, Computer,
          Account, ShareName, ShareUser,
          NewProcessName, ParentProcessName, CommandLine
| order by PivotTime desc
ucs.rationale: Sygnia's two Velvet Ant write-ups (2024A + 2024B) document the actor's signature behaviour — using a long-compromised internal F5 BIG-IP (or other unmonitored appliance) as a hop point to re-enter the Windows estate after EDR remediation, then using SMB and WMI to redeploy PlugX. Generic 'WMI lateral movement' or 'unusual port' detections each fire constantly; chaining them — inbound from an appli
